# Example Caddyfile for a Coterie deployment.
#
# Copy or include this in your /etc/caddy/Caddyfile and replace
# `coterie.example.com` and `example.com` with your real hostnames.
# Caddy provisions and renews TLS certificates automatically; no other
# config is required for HTTPS.
#
# Two virtual hosts:
#   1. coterie.example.com — the Coterie API + management portal
#   2. example.com         — your public marketing / member-signup site
#                            (static files; can also be hosted on a
#                             separate provider if you'd rather)
#
# Reload after editing:
#   sudo caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
#   sudo systemctl reload caddy
#
# Logs:
#   sudo journalctl -u caddy -f               (Caddy itself)
#   sudo tail -f /var/log/caddy/coterie-access.log

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Coterie portal + API
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
coterie.example.com {
    # Reverse proxy to the local Coterie binary. Coterie listens on
    # 127.0.0.1:8080 by default; only Caddy can reach it.
    reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:8080

    # Compression. zstd is preferred when supported; gzip is the
    # fallback for older clients. Coterie also serves a CompressionLayer
    # internally — Caddy's encode wins because it sits in front, so the
    # internal one is effectively a no-op for HTTPS clients.
    encode zstd gzip

    # Hardening headers that complement the ones Coterie sets itself.
    # Coterie sets CSP (with per-request nonce), X-Content-Type-Options,
    # X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy. The ones below are the
    # transport-layer ones Coterie can't reasonably set without
    # knowing the deployment topology.
    header {
        # 1 year HSTS, including subdomains, with preload eligibility.
        # Only enable preload after you've confirmed every subdomain
        # of `example.com` is HTTPS-ready and you actually want to be
        # in the browser preload list.
        Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"

        # Don't advertise software fingerprints in 4xx/5xx pages.
        -Server
        -X-Powered-By
    }

    # Access logging. Rotate this file with logrotate or via Caddy's
    # built-in log rotation (uncomment the `roll_*` lines).
    log {
        output file /var/log/caddy/coterie-access.log {
            # roll_size 100MiB
            # roll_keep 7
            # roll_keep_for 720h
        }
        format json
    }

    # Stripe webhook bodies can be slightly larger than typical API
    # requests (especially payment_intent.succeeded with full PM
    # metadata). 1 MB is a comfortable ceiling.
    request_body {
        max_size 1MB
    }
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Public marketing / signup site (optional)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Use this block if you're hosting the public site from the same VPS.
# If your public site lives on Netlify / Cloudflare Pages / etc.,
# delete this block.
example.com, www.example.com {
    root * /var/www/example.com
    encode zstd gzip
    file_server

    # Match coterie's HSTS so the apex domain is also locked to HTTPS.
    header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"

    log {
        output file /var/log/caddy/site-access.log
        format json
    }
}
